paragraphs.text: Axeptio is a French start-up founded in 2017, specialising in Consent Management Platform interfaces. The company is based in Montpellier, France, and has developed in Europe and internationally through several acquisitions. Since 2021 Axeptio has raised EUR 5.1 million.

Axeptio warmly welcomed the European Commission’s proposal to better regulate the digital space through the upcoming legislation on digital fairness. Based on its experience in the field of online consent gathering, Axeptio wishes to share its views and recommendations with the Commission.
Banning any idea of a standardised interface, fatally leading to fatigue of consent.

section_path: Free movement of personal data: new “load-bearing wall” of European integration

paragraphs.text: Two main principles constitute ‘load-bearing walls’ of the European Union: free movement of persons and free movement of goods and services. The GDPR has created a third axis: free movement of personal data.

paragraphs.text: Here is a key recommendation from the Tricot report, which Axeptio supports in the context of the digital fairness legislation: increased education to raise awareness of these issues. The report deplores "the great silence in the lessons learned about the general design of IT" (p. 79). It calls for multidisciplinary education that integrates the legal, social and ethical dimensions of IT, so that it is used in an informed manner that respects fundamental rights.
